title: Explicit Literals: Control Characters and Whitespace
description: Non-encoded control characters and whitespace characters should be explicit in literals to avoid invisible or difficult-to-recognize issues that can result in bugs. Use their encoded version (ASCII or Unicode).
recommendation: Use encoded versions of control characters and whitespace in literals.
impact: Improves code quality and security by preventing unexpected behavior due to invisible or difficult-to-recognize characters.

checks.message: Use encoded versions of control characters and whitespace in literals.
